title: Strategic Planner
description: Lead long-term planning excellence — master the full S&OP cycle and the planning software that drives it, from demand and supply scenarios through to executive decision-making.

keyAreas: The end-to-end operating model: where S&OP sits relative to S&OE and execution, and how the three horizons connect
Who owns strategic planning in the organisation and how it interfaces with commercial, finance, and supply functions
Why data quality is the foundation of credible long-range plans
What planning software is and how it enables structured, data-driven S&OP
The S&OP cycle in depth: phases, key decisions, outputs, and cross-functional ownership
Scenario planning fundamentals: what scenarios are, when to use them, and how they feed S&OP
Planning software data objects, the network model, and the demand/supply/inventory logic that underpins plans
RASCI accountability across demand, supply, and executive S&OP reviews
Data types, quality standards, and the data model from source systems through to plan
How planning software, ERP, and other systems integrate end-to-end
Running the full S&OP cycle: demand review, supply review, inventory review, resource review, and executive S&OP
Hands-on scenario building and comparison in planning software
Simulating constrained plans, running what-if scenarios, and promoting the right plan
Navigating planning software to read and challenge plan output
Demand forecasting: statistical baselines, commercial overlays, and consensus building
Setting and owning planning policy: safety stock targets, service level agreements, and allocation rules
Governance and escalation: how strategic planning decisions are surfaced, challenged, and closed
KPI ownership: which long-horizon metrics you own and how to steer on them in S&OP reviews
Data governance: maintaining master data quality as the foundation of credible long-range plans
